topic_title: The four properties of a good algorithm — finite, definite, input, output

required_sections.detail: Sections 3 (Learning Objectives ~90 words), 4 (Introduction ~200 words), 5 (Core Concepts ~860 words), and 10 (Key Takeaways ~130 words) all present and non-trivial. All minimum thresholds met (section 5 ≥100 words; sections 3, 4, 10 ≥50 words).

citation_density.detail: Primary resource [1] cited multiple times throughout all content sections. All three resources cited in sections 5, 6, and 7. Approximately 78% of standalone factual assertions carry a citation marker, exceeding the 60% threshold. A small number of assertions lack citations (e.g., 'Infinite loops are one of the most common bugs in programming', 'A computer cannot guess at meaning', 'Key point: Every step should bring it closer to finishing') — these are minor soft items, not hard fails.
soft_findings: 3–4 factual assertions in section 5 lack a citation marker but do not constitute a hard fail.

The word 'program' (used informally in section 5.1: 'the program runs forever') has not been formally introduced in any prior topic's concepts_introduced. Used colloquially, not defined — borderline but acceptable.
The word 'server' (section 7: 'when a server is slow') has not been introduced in any prior topic. Used in a brief aside, not defined — acceptable.
sequence_drift.detail: All bolded/formally introduced terms are in the allowed set for topics 1.1–1.11. Cross-topic references (topic 1.9 'unambiguous steps', topic 1.1 'input/process/output model', topic 1.10 'stopping condition') are explicitly flagged with topic numbers. No hard forward references detected.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated word count ~2300 is below the standard profile target range of 3000–4400 words (23% below the lower bound). The SKILL.md hard-fail threshold is >30% outside the range. At 23% below, this is within the tolerance band — a soft warning rather than a hard fail. The corpus is above the absolute floor of 1500 words. Flagged for the human reviewer at HITL #2 to consider requesting expansion, particularly in sections 5 (Core Concepts) and 7 (Real-World Patterns).

topic_scope.detail: Corpus stays tightly within the headline scope of the four properties (finiteness, definiteness, input, output). No Big-O complexity, no algorithm efficiency or effectiveness as a 5th property, no NP-completeness, no time/space complexity. The term 'well-defined algorithm' is correctly listed in this topic's concepts_introduced and is appropriate to introduce here. No scope creep detected.

beginner_readability.detail: All four main properties (finiteness, definiteness, input as algorithm property, output as algorithm property) are bolded and defined in plain language on first use. 'infinite loop' and 'termination' and 'well-defined algorithm' are similarly defined on first appearance. No jargon dumps (no paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously — each section introduces one property). Register is plain, direct, and active throughout. No unexplained technical acronyms (GPS/ATM/PIN are everyday terms). Sentence length is generally appropriate for zero-background learners. No readability hard-fail threshold reached.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. Soft warnings: (1) corpus length (~2300 words) is below the standard profile 3000–4400 word target by ~23% — not a hard fail but the human reviewer at HITL #2 should consider requesting expansion in Core Concepts and Real-World Patterns; (2) 3–4 factual assertions in section 5 lack citation markers (still above 60% threshold); (3) 'program' and 'server' used informally without formal introduction in prior topics — colloquial use only, no hard fail.
notes_for_human_reviewer: The corpus is well-structured and beginner-appropriate. Primary concern is that estimated word count (~2300) is below the standard depth target of 3000–4400 words. Recommend expanding section 5 subsections (each core concept could use one more concrete example) and section 7 (one additional real-world pattern). This will also strengthen the reading artifact generated downstream.
checks_summary: required_sections → PASS: all four sections present and above word minimums
citation_density → PASS: primary cited throughout; ~78% of claims in sections 5-7 carry citation markers; 3-4 uncited assertions (soft only)
sequence_drift → PASS: no hard forward references; 'program' and 'server' used informally (soft)
length_sanity → SOFT WARNING: ~2300 words vs 3000–4400 target (23% below lower bound, within 30% hard-fail threshold)
topic_scope → PASS: no scope creep; corpus stays on the four properties only
beginner_readability → PASS: all terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, plain register, 0 hard findings
